title: Getting started with PTSDdiag
text: PTSD defined DSM-5-TR requires presence symptoms across four clusters, drawing total 20 symptoms. polythetic, hierarchical structure theoretically permits 636,000 symptom combinations qualifying diagnosis, yet empirical research shown patients cluster around limited number distinct profiles. complexity full criteria creates substantial assessment burden contributes diagnostic heterogeneity complicates clinical practice research. PTSDdiag package implements computational optimization approach systematically searches possible subsets given size, identifies combinations minimize misclassification relative full DSM-5-TR criteria, evaluates robustness results across multiple high-performing solutions. vignette demonstrates complete workflow scored PCL-5 dataset optimized symptom combinations summary output.

Four caveats worth mentioning. Items matched position, name. first non-identifier column becomes symptom_1, second becomes symptom_2, . item columns must therefore already standard DSM-5-TR order (intrusive memories first, sleep disturbance last). data may contain 20 items columns name id_col. matching positional, function distinguish stray column (item another questionnaire, date) PCL-5 item. Rather risk shifting every item one position, stops error whenever number non-identifier columns exactly 20. List columns want keep (e.g., identifiers, demographics) id_col, remove anything else calling. Items scoring needs follow PCL-5 convention. Items need scored 0 4, symptom counts present score 2 higher, following DSM-5-TR. Missing data permitted. Missing data handled explicitly stopping function otherwise bias results.

title: Find optimal non-hierarchical six-symptom combinations for PTSD diagnosis — analyze_best_six_symptoms_four_required
text: function: Tests possible combinations 6 symptoms 20 PCL-5 items Requires 4 symptoms present (>=2 original 0-4 scale) diagnosis Identifies three combinations best match original DSM-5 diagnosis Optimization can based either: Minimizing false cases (false positives false negatives) Minimizing false negatives (newly non-diagnosed cases) symptom clusters PCL-5 : Items 1-5: Intrusion symptoms (Criterion B) Items 6-7: Avoidance symptoms (Criterion C) Items 8-14: Negative alterations cognitions mood (Criterion D) Items 15-20: Alterations arousal reactivity (Criterion E)

what: Apply pre-specified symptom combinations to new data — apply_symptom_combinations
title: Apply pre-specified symptom combinations to new data — apply_symptom_combinations
text: Applies one pre-specified symptom combinations new dataset returns comparison dataframe baseline DSM-5 diagnosis simplified diagnosis combination. function enables external validation allowing combinations derived one dataset tested another.

title: Apply pre-specified symptom combinations to new data — apply_symptom_combinations
code: apply_symptom_combinations(data, combinations, n_required = 4, clusters = NULL)

title: Apply pre-specified symptom combinations to new data — apply_symptom_combinations
text: data dataframe containing exactly 20 columns PCL-5 item scores   (output rename_ptsd_columns). symptom scored   0-4 scale : 0 = 1 = little bit 2 = Moderately 3 = Quite bit 4 = Extremely combinations list integer vectors. vector contains symptom indices (e.g., c(4, 6, 7, 17, 19, 20)). vectors must length. Typically obtained results$best_symptoms optimization run. n_required Integer specifying many symptoms must present (scored >= 2 original scale) positive diagnosis (default: 4). Must 1 length combination. clusters NULL (default) non-hierarchical checking,   named list integer vectors defining cluster structure   hierarchical checking. provided, positive diagnosis additionally   requires present symptoms span defined clusters. PCL-5:   list(B = 1:5, C = 6:7, D = 8:14, E = 15:20)

title: Apply pre-specified symptom combinations to new data — apply_symptom_combinations
text: dataframe columns: carry-columns data (e.g. ID column added     via rename_ptsd_columns), prepended original order. PTSD_orig: Logical. Full DSM-5 diagnosis computed data. One column per combination (logical): Simplified diagnosis     combination. Column names follow symptom_X_Y_Z pattern. dataframe can passed directly summarize_ptsd_changes compute diagnostic accuracy metrics.

title: Apply pre-specified symptom combinations to new data — apply_symptom_combinations
text: function: Computes baseline DSM-5 diagnosis (PTSD_orig)     provided data using binarized scores Binarizes symptom data (scores >= 2 become 1, others become 0) combination, determines diagnosis based whether least     n_required symptoms present clusters provided, additionally checks present     symptoms span defined clusters (hierarchical checking) Returns comparison dataframe suitable     summarize_ptsd_changes Typical workflow external validation: Derive optimal combinations Dataset using     optimize_combinations     optimize_combinations_clusters Apply combinations Dataset B using function Compute diagnostic metrics using     summarize_ptsd_changes     create_readable_summary

title: Apply pre-specified symptom combinations to new data — apply_symptom_combinations
code: # Create example data set.seed(42) ptsd_data <- data.frame(matrix(sample(0:4, 400, replace=TRUE), ncol=20)) names(ptsd_data) <- paste0("symptom_", 1:20)  # Define some combinations to test my_combinations <- list(   c(1, 6, 8, 10, 15, 19),   c(2, 7, 9, 11, 16, 20) )  # Apply combinations (non-hierarchical) comparison <- apply_symptom_combinations(ptsd_data, my_combinations,                 n_required = 4)  # Compute metrics metrics <- summarize_ptsd_changes(comparison) create_readable_summary(metrics) #>                 Scenario Total Diagnosed Total Non-Diagnosed True Positive #> 1              PTSD_orig        11 (55%)             9 (45%)            11 #> 2 symptom_1_6_8_10_15_19         9 (45%)            11 (55%)             8 #> 3 symptom_2_7_9_11_16_20         9 (45%)            11 (55%)             7 #>   True Negative Newly Diagnosed Newly Non-Diagnosed True Cases False Cases #> 1             9               0                   0         20           0 #> 2             8               1                   3         16           4 #> 3             7               2                   4         14           6 #>   Sensitivity Specificity    PPV    NPV Accuracy #> 1      1.0000      1.0000 1.0000 1.0000      1.0 #> 2      0.7273      0.8889 0.8889 0.7273      0.8 #> 3      0.6364      0.7778 0.7778 0.6364      0.7  # Apply with hierarchical checking pcl5_clusters <- list(B = 1:5, C = 6:7, D = 8:14, E = 15:20) hier_comparison <- apply_symptom_combinations(ptsd_data, my_combinations,                      n_required = 4, clusters = pcl5_clusters) summarize_ptsd_changes(hier_comparison) #>                                        column diagnosed non_diagnosed #> PTSD_orig                           PTSD_orig        11             9 #> symptom_1_6_8_10_15_19 symptom_1_6_8_10_15_19         1            19 #> symptom_2_7_9_11_16_20 symptom_2_7_9_11_16_20         3            17 #>                        newly_diagnosed newly_nondiagnosed true_positive #> PTSD_orig                            0                  0            11 #> symptom_1_6_8_10_15_19               0                 10             1 #> symptom_2_7_9_11_16_20               0                  8             3 #>                        true_negative true_cases false_cases sensitivity #> PTSD_orig                          9         20           0  1.00000000 #> symptom_1_6_8_10_15_19             9         10          10  0.09090909 #> symptom_2_7_9_11_16_20             9         12           8  0.27272727 #>                        specificity ppv       npv diagnosed_percent #> PTSD_orig                        1   1 1.0000000                55 #> symptom_1_6_8_10_15_19           1   1 0.4736842                 5 #> symptom_2_7_9_11_16_20           1   1 0.5294118                15 #>                        non_diagnosed_percent accuracy #> PTSD_orig                                 45      1.0 #> symptom_1_6_8_10_15_19                    95      0.5 #> symptom_2_7_9_11_16_20                    85      0.6

what: Compare multiple diagnostic systems against a reference standard — compare_diagnostic_systems
title: Compare multiple diagnostic systems against a reference standard — compare_diagnostic_systems
text: Produces single unified summary table comparing diagnostic performance multiple criteria chosen reference standard. Suitable use manuscript table comparing optimized symptom combinations, ICD-11, CAPS-5, DSM-5-TR one kable-ready output.

title: Compare multiple diagnostic systems against a reference standard — compare_diagnostic_systems
code: compare_diagnostic_systems(   data,   ...,   icd11 = TRUE,   caps5_data = NULL,   reference = c("pcl5", "caps5"),   labels = NULL )

title: Compare multiple diagnostic systems against a reference standard — compare_diagnostic_systems
text: data dataframe containing exactly 20 columns PCL-5 item scores (output rename_ptsd_columns). Always required. Used compute PCL-5 DSM-5-TR diagnosis , icd11 = TRUE, ICD-11 diagnosis. ... Zero comparison dataframes, containing PTSD_orig column least one additional logical column representing diagnostic system (e.g. output apply_symptom_combinations). caps5_data NULL, PTSD_orig columns must identical one computed data. caps5_data provided, row counts validated. icd11 Logical. TRUE (default), compute ICD-11 PTSD diagnosis data include row output. caps5_data Optional dataframe containing exactly 20 columns CAPS-5 item severity scores (output rename_caps5_columns). Must number rows data (paired participants). provided, CAPS-5 DSM-5-TR diagnosis computed internally included comparison. reference Character. DSM-5-TR diagnosis serves reference standard: "pcl5" (default) "caps5". reference row always sensitivity = specificity = 1 zero misclassifications. Setting reference = "caps5" requires caps5_data provided. labels Optional character vector display names systems coming ..., order columns appear across ... inputs (excluding PTSD_orig columns). apply built-rows (DSM-5-TR, ICD-11, CAPS-5), always labelled automatically. NULL (default), column names used. warning issued length match.

title: Compare multiple diagnostic systems against a reference standard — compare_diagnostic_systems
text: data.frame one row per diagnostic system   following columns: system: Display name diagnostic criterion n_diagnosed: Number cases meeting criterion pct_diagnosed: Percentage total sample diagnosed (2 dp) sensitivity: 4 dp specificity: 4 dp ppv: Positive predictive value, 4 dp npv: Negative predictive value, 4 dp n_false_negative: Cases missed vs. reference pct_false_negative: Percentage total sample, 2 dp n_false_positive: Cases -diagnosed vs. reference pct_false_positive: Percentage total sample, 2 dp n_misclassified: Total misclassified cases accuracy: Proportion classified reference     ((total - misclassified) / total), 4 dp

title: Compare multiple diagnostic systems against a reference standard — compare_diagnostic_systems
text: function: Computes PCL-5 DSM-5-TR diagnosis data caps5_data provided, computes CAPS-5 DSM-5-TR     diagnosis Sets reference standard based reference: either     PCL-5 CAPS-5 DSM-5-TR diagnosis. reference row always appears     first sensitivity = specificity = 1. Optionally computes ICD-11 diagnosis data     icd11 = TRUE Collects non-PTSD_orig columns ...     comparison dataframes (e.g. output     apply_symptom_combinations) Calls summarize_ptsd_changes internally reshapes     result presentation-ready table caps5_data NULL (default), labels follow original convention: "DSM-5-TR" "ICD-11". caps5_data provided, labels disambiguated instrument name: "DSM-5-TR (PCL-5)", "DSM-5-TR (CAPS-5)", "ICD-11 (PCL-5)". caps5_data provided, strict PTSD_orig validation ... inputs relaxed row-count check , comparison dataframes may derived either PCL-5 CAPS-5 data (produce different PTSD_orig vectors).

title: Compare multiple diagnostic systems against a reference standard — compare_diagnostic_systems

title: Run multiple PTSD optimization scenarios in one call — compare_optimizations
text: Runs several optimization scenarios dataset bundles results single object suitable tabular visual comparison. Reproduces multi-scenario workflow used PTSDdiag preprint (4/6 hierarchical, 4/6 non-hierarchical, 3/6 non-hierarchical) one call, also supports adding fixed criteria ICD-11 comparison.

title: Run multiple PTSD optimization scenarios in one call — compare_optimizations
code: compare_optimizations(   data,   scenarios = NULL,   include_icd11 = FALSE,   n_top = 10,   score_by = "accuracy",   clusters = NULL,   show_progress = TRUE )

title: Run multiple PTSD optimization scenarios in one call — compare_optimizations
text: data dataframe containing 20 PCL-5 item columns symptom_1 symptom_20 (output rename_ptsd_columns). Non-symptom columns (e.g. participant identifier) carried every scenario's per-row diagnosis output. scenarios Optional named list scenario configurations.   element list :  type: "optimize" (default omitted)       "fixed". type = "optimize": n_symptoms (integer 1-20),       n_required (integer 1-n_symptoms), hierarchical       (single logical), optional clusters (named list integer       vectors; defaults PCL-5 B/C/D/E hierarchical = TRUE). type = "fixed": criterion – either known       string ("icd11" "caps5") logical vector       length nrow(data) representing pre-computed diagnosis.       supplying logical vector must also provide symptoms,       integer indices counted "included" heatmap. NULL (default), runs three preprint scenarios: 4/6   hierarchical, 4/6 non-hierarchical, 3/6 non-hierarchical. include_icd11 Logical. TRUE, appends "ICD-11" fixed-criterion scenario user-supplied entries (deduplicated label). Default FALSE. n_top Integer. Number top combinations retain per optimize scenario (default 10). Fixed scenarios always contribute exactly one combination regardless n_top. score_by Character. Optimization criterion: "accuracy" (minimise FP + FN) "sensitivity" (minimise FN ). Applied optimize scenarios override . Default "accuracy". clusters Optional named list integer vectors defining PCL-5 clusters used hierarchical optimize scenarios specify . Defaults DSM-5 B/C/D/E grouping needed. show_progress Logical. Forwarded optimize scenario's progress bar. Default TRUE.

title: Run multiple PTSD optimization scenarios in one call — compare_optimizations
text: object class ptsdiag_comparison, list : scenarios: named list per-scenario results. element     mirrors shape returned optimize_combinations     (best_symptoms, diagnosis_comparison, summary,     n_tied) carries type attribute. config: data.frame one row per scenario summarising     configuration used. n_rows: number input rows. call: matched call. Pass result summarize_top_combinations manuscript-ready performance table, symptom_frequency long-format symptom inclusion counts, plot_symptom_frequency heatmap.

title: Run multiple PTSD optimization scenarios in one call — compare_optimizations
text: scenario either: optimize (default): runs optimize_combinations     optimize_combinations_clusters depending     hierarchical. Returns top n_top combinations. fixed: applies pre-defined diagnostic criterion (    ICD-11 PTSD) treats fixed symptom set single "combination"     purpose multi-scenario tables heatmap. Fixed scenarios let researchers benchmark optimized criteria published systems uniform output. non-symptom columns present data (e.g. ID column added via rename_ptsd_columns(..., id_col = "patient_id")) carried scenario's per-row diagnosis_comparison, per-participant diagnoses can joined back demographics.

title: Compute CAPS-5 DSM-5-TR PTSD diagnosis — create_caps5_diagnosis
text: Applies DSM-5-TR PTSD diagnostic algorithm CAPS-5 (Clinician-Administered PTSD Scale DSM-5) item scores returns single-column dataframe indicating diagnostic status. CAPS-5 uses 20-item structure, 0–4 severity scale, \(\ge\) 2 symptom-presence threshold PCL-5, diagnostic algorithm identical.

title: Compute CAPS-5 DSM-5-TR PTSD diagnosis — create_caps5_diagnosis
code: create_caps5_diagnosis(data)

title: Compute CAPS-5 DSM-5-TR PTSD diagnosis — create_caps5_diagnosis
text: data dataframe containing exactly 20 columns CAPS-5 item severity scores (output rename_caps5_columns). Columns must named symptom_1 symptom_20, scored 0–4 scale, missing values.

title: Compute CAPS-5 DSM-5-TR PTSD diagnosis — create_caps5_diagnosis
text: data.frame one logical column one row per   participant: PTSD_caps5: CAPS-5 DSM-5-TR diagnosis carry-columns present data (e.g. ID column added via rename_caps5_columns) prepended original order.

title: Compute CAPS-5 DSM-5-TR PTSD diagnosis — create_caps5_diagnosis
text: DSM-5-TR diagnostic criteria applied : Criterion B (Intrusion): \(\ge\) 1 items 1–5 severity     \(\ge\) 2 Criterion C (Avoidance): \(\ge\) 1 items 6–7 severity     \(\ge\) 2 Criterion D (Negative cognitions/mood): \(\ge\) 2 items 8–14     severity \(\ge\) 2 Criterion E (Arousal/reactivity): \(\ge\) 2 items 15–20     severity \(\ge\) 2 four criteria must met positive diagnosis. Unlike create_icd11_diagnosis, function returns CAPS-5 diagnosis column (PTSD_caps5), PCL-5 reference column. CAPS-5 diagnosis typically used gold-standard reference , compared PCL-5 baseline. returned dataframe can passed compare_diagnostic_systems via caps5_data parameter, used directly descriptive analyses.

title: Compute CAPS-5 DSM-5-TR PTSD diagnosis — create_caps5_diagnosis
code: # Simulate CAPS-5 data (using same structure as PCL-5) set.seed(42) caps5_raw <- data.frame(matrix(sample(0:4, 400, replace = TRUE), ncol = 20)) caps5_data <- rename_caps5_columns(caps5_raw) caps5_dx <- create_caps5_diagnosis(caps5_data) head(caps5_dx) #>   PTSD_caps5 #> 1      FALSE #> 2      FALSE #> 3       TRUE #> 4       TRUE #> 5      FALSE #> 6      FALSE table(caps5_dx$PTSD_caps5) #>  #> FALSE  TRUE  #>     9    11

text: data.frame two logical columns one row per   participant: PTSD_orig: DSM-5-TR diagnosis (reference standard) PTSD_icd11: ICD-11 diagnosis carry-columns present data (e.g. ID column added via rename_ptsd_columns) prepended original order results can joined back source dataframe. dataframe can passed directly summarize_ptsd_changes used input compare_diagnostic_systems.

title: Apply ICD-11 PTSD diagnostic criteria to PCL-5 data — create_icd11_diagnosis
text: ICD-11 PTSD requires THREE following clusters met (symptom present = score \(\ge\) 2 original 0–4 scale): Re-experiencing (present): \(\ge\) 1 PCL-5     items 1, 2, 3 (intrusive memories, nightmares, flashbacks) Avoidance: \(\ge\) 1 PCL-5 items 6, 7 Sense current threat: \(\ge\) 1 PCL-5 items 16, 17     (hypervigilance, exaggerated startle) minimum 3 symptoms total across ICD-11 items (1, 2, 3, 6, 7, 16, 17) must present. automatically satisfied three cluster requirements met enforced explicitly clarity. DSM-5-TR diagnosis (PTSD_orig) computed using binarization logic rest package (create_ptsd_diagnosis_binarized).

title: Apply ICD-11 PTSD diagnostic criteria to PCL-5 data — create_icd11_diagnosis
code: # Apply ICD-11 criteria to the built-in simulated dataset ptsd_data <- rename_ptsd_columns(simulated_ptsd,                                   id_col = c("patient_id", "age", "sex")) icd11_result <- create_icd11_diagnosis(ptsd_data) head(icd11_result) #>   patient_id age    sex PTSD_orig PTSD_icd11 #> 1      P0001  48   male      TRUE       TRUE #> 2      P0002  29   male      TRUE       TRUE #> 3      P0003  44   male      TRUE      FALSE #> 4      P0004  41 female      TRUE       TRUE #> 5      P0005  34   male      TRUE       TRUE #> 6      P0006  18   male      TRUE       TRUE  # Feed directly into the metrics pipeline metrics <- summarize_ptsd_changes(icd11_result) create_readable_summary(metrics) #>     Scenario Total Diagnosed Total Non-Diagnosed True Positive True Negative #> 1  PTSD_orig    4710 (94.2%)          290 (5.8%)          4710           290 #> 2 PTSD_icd11   4641 (92.82%)         359 (7.18%)          4607           256 #>   Newly Diagnosed Newly Non-Diagnosed True Cases False Cases Sensitivity #> 1               0                   0       5000           0      1.0000 #> 2              34                 103       4863         137      0.9781 #>   Specificity    PPV    NPV Accuracy #> 1      1.0000 1.0000 1.0000   1.0000 #> 2      0.8828 0.9927 0.7131   0.9726

text: data dataframe : column represents different diagnostic criterion Must include column named "PTSD_orig" baseline criterion Values logical (TRUE/FALSE) indicating whether PTSD criteria met row represents one case/participant

title: Summarize changes in PTSD diagnostic metrics — summarize_ptsd_changes
text: dataframe containing following columns diagnostic criterion: column: Name diagnostic criterion diagnosed: Number cases diagnosed PTSD non_diagnosed: Number cases diagnosed PTSD diagnosed_percent: Percentage cases diagnosed non_diagnosed_percent: Percentage cases diagnosed newly_diagnosed: Cases diagnosed new baseline criterion     (false positive) newly_nondiagnosed: Cases diagnosed baseline new criterion     (false negative) true_positive: Cases diagnosed criteria true_negative: Cases diagnosed either criterion true_cases: Sum true positives true negatives false_cases: Sum newly diagnosed (false positive) newly non-diagnosed     (false negative) sensitivity, specificity, ppv, npv: Standard diagnostic accuracy metrics accuracy: Proportion cases classified baseline     criterion ((true positives + true negatives) / total)

title: Summarize changes in PTSD diagnostic metrics — summarize_ptsd_changes
text: function calculates multiple diagnostic metrics comparing diagnostic criterion baseline criterion (PTSD_orig): Basic counts: Number percentage diagnosed/non-diagnosed cases per criterion Number newly diagnosed (false positive) newly non-diagnosed   (false negative) cases True positive true negative cases Diagnostic accuracy metrics: Sensitivity: Proportion true PTSD cases correctly identified Specificity: Proportion non-PTSD cases correctly identified PPV (Positive Predictive Value): Probability positive diagnosis correct NPV (Negative Predictive Value): Probability negative diagnosis correct

title: Summarize changes in PTSD diagnostic metrics — summarize_ptsd_changes
code: # Create sample diagnostic data set.seed(123) n_cases <- 100 sample_data <- data.frame(   PTSD_orig = sample(c(TRUE, FALSE), n_cases, replace = TRUE),   PTSD_alt1 = sample(c(TRUE, FALSE), n_cases, replace = TRUE),   PTSD_alt2 = sample(c(TRUE, FALSE), n_cases, replace = TRUE) )  # Calculate diagnostic metrics diagnostic_metrics <- summarize_ptsd_changes(sample_data) diagnostic_metrics #>              column diagnosed non_diagnosed newly_diagnosed newly_nondiagnosed #> PTSD_orig PTSD_orig        57            43               0                  0 #> PTSD_alt1 PTSD_alt1        46            54              20                 31 #> PTSD_alt2 PTSD_alt2        51            49              26                 32 #>           true_positive true_negative true_cases false_cases sensitivity #> PTSD_orig            57            43        100           0   1.0000000 #> PTSD_alt1            26            23         49          51   0.4561404 #> PTSD_alt2            25            17         42          58   0.4385965 #>           specificity       ppv       npv diagnosed_percent #> PTSD_orig   1.0000000 1.0000000 1.0000000                57 #> PTSD_alt1   0.5348837 0.5652174 0.4259259                46 #> PTSD_alt2   0.3953488 0.4901961 0.3469388                51 #>           non_diagnosed_percent accuracy #> PTSD_orig                    43     1.00 #> PTSD_alt1                    54     0.49 #> PTSD_alt2                    49     0.42

title: PTSDdiag 0.2.0
text: New generalized optimization functions optimize_combinations() optimize_combinations_clusters() allow configurable number symptoms per combination (n_symptoms), required threshold (n_required), number top results (n_top), custom cluster definitions (clusters). New apply_symptom_combinations() function external validation: apply pre-derived combinations new dataset compare DSM-5 baseline. New write_combinations() read_combinations() functions exporting importing optimized symptom combinations human-readable JSON files, enabling collaboration across research groups without sharing raw data. Original functions analyze_best_six_symptoms_four_required() analyze_best_six_symptoms_four_required_clusters() preserved backward-compatible wrappers PCL-5 defaults. holdout_validation() cross_validation() now accept n_symptoms, n_required, n_top parameters. Internal refactoring: shared validation diagnosis logic consolidated reusable internal helpers.

title: PTSDdiag 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-02-13 Initial CRAN submission. Core analysis functions identifying optimal 6-symptom PTSD diagnostic combinations using PCL-5 data. Hierarchical (cluster-based) non-hierarchical analysis approaches. DSM-5 diagnostic criteria implementation (binarized non-binarized). Diagnostic accuracy metrics: sensitivity, specificity, PPV, NPV. Holdout k-fold cross-validation methods model evaluation. Simulated PCL-5 dataset 5,000 observations demonstration.
